Article Title: 'Does India run on your arrogance?' BJP tears into Rahul Gandhi over PM residence march
Article Text:
BJP calls Rahul's march to PM residence 'irresponsible conduct'

NEW DELHI: A day after Rahul Gandhi's march to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence ended in a confrontation with police, the BJP mounted a sharp political counteroffensive on Wednesday, accusing Lok Sabha leader of opposition of disregarding the dignity of the Prime Minister's residence and announcing nationwide protests against Congress party.The saffron party also called out Rahul Gandhi's "Chalo PM Residence" as "irresponsible" and "highly condemnable." Leading the attack, senior party leader Ravi Shankar Prasad asked Lok Sabha LoP: "'Does this country run on your arrogance?"Addressing a presser in New Delhi, senior BJP leader and former Union minister Ravi Shankar Prasad took a swipe at Rahul Gandhi's political legacy, saying that someone from a family that produced three Prime Ministers should have understood the dignity attached to the Prime Minister's residence."He is sitting on a dharna at the gate of the Prime Minister's residence. His father Rajiv Gandhi was the Prime Minister, his grandmother Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister, and his great-grandfather Jawaharlal Nehru was also the Prime Minister of India. He, of all people, should know the dignity and sanctity of the Prime Minister's residence. Despite this, the kind of irresponsible behaviour he has displayed is highly condemnable," Prasad said.Announcing the BJP's response, Prasad said the saffron party would stage protests across the country outside Congress offices."The BJP will hold protests today across the country against Congress outside Congress offices for the unruly protest outside the PM's house yesterday," he said.Questioning Rahul Gandhi's approach, Prasad accused the Congress leader of abandoning dialogue and trying to dictate terms to the government."How do you intend to function? Will you just sit there forcibly? You were engaged in a dialogue. They were ready for a debate on the NEET issue, but then you backed out. Does this country run on your stick? Will it run on your arrogance? Rahul Gandhi, you have been irresponsible. I condemn your entire conduct," he said.Defending the police action during the protest, the BJP leader said security personnel had no option but to intervene to maintain law and order in the high-security zone."Later, the police were forced to take action, which was necessary for security and for maintaining peace. The Prime Minister has always been clear on these issues," Prasad said.Shifting focus to the controversy surrounding competitive examinations, Prasad asserted that the government had acted swiftly to protect the integrity of recruitment and entrance tests."In the past, irregularities were reported in several states during various examinations. Consequently, foolproof exams were conducted immediately after cancellations. The results have been declared with a 56-57% success rate, and students are satisfied. These exams were held without charging any additional fees," he told reporters.Emphasising the Centre's commitment to students, Prasad said those responsible for exam irregularities had been arrested and were facing trial."All the candidates appearing for NEET and other exams are our children. It is our duty to be concerned about their future, and we will fulfill that responsibility. Those involved in irregularities have been arrested and are facing trial. The government is ensuring that their trial leads to certain punishment," he said.The senior BJP leader concluded by reiterating Prime Minister Narendra Modi's assurance that the sanctity of NEET and other examinations would be protected through systemic reforms and stricter scrutiny.The BJP's attack came a day after Rahul led the Opposition's march towards Lok Kalyan Marg, the official residence of the Prime Minister, where protesters came face-to-face with security personnel in the high-security Lutyens' Delhi zone.Videos from the scene showed police moving in to disperse the protesters gathered outside the Prime Minister's residence. In one video shared by the Congress on its official X handle, Rahul Gandhi was seen lying on the road as police personnel attempted to lift and remove him from the protest site. The former Congress president appeared to resist being taken away as security officials surrounded him.The Congress also released a photograph showing Rahul nosebleed following the police action, alleging excessive use of force during the protest.Rahul Gandhi's protest came amid Jantar Mantar protest demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over accountability in the NEET paper-leak.
Article Summary: Bjp calls rahul's march to pm residence 'irresponsible conduct' party leader ravi shankar prasad asks rahul: "does this country run on your arrogance? " He says security personnel had no option but to intervene to maintain law and order. The former congress president appeared to resist being taken away as security officials surrounded him.

Article Title: Daughter in SC, seeks return of Netaji's ashes from Japan
Article Text:
Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose ’s octogenarian Germany-based daughter, Anita Pfaff, moved Supreme Court

NEW DELHI: Three months after a failed attempt by his grandnephew, Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose's octogenarian Germany-based daughter, Anita Pfaff, moved Supreme Court Tuesday seeking a direction to govt to bring his ashes back from the Renko-Ji temple in Japan.Pfaff's petition, settled by senior advocate A M Singhvi, complained of "India's prolonged failure to take a final, reasoned and time-bound decision" to repatriate to India the "mortal remains/ashes attributed to Netaji, preserved at Renko-Ji temple" to allow the sole living direct lineal descendant to perform his last rites with dignity and finality."There is gross delay," said a bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi. However, it adjourned the hearing on Pfaff's petition on finding the arguing counsel absent.Ahead of the West Bengal assembly elections, a petition was filed in SC by Netaji's gr-andnephew, Ashis Ray, seeking the same direction to the Centre. Appearing for Ray, Singhvi had argued before the bench of the same three judges on March 12 that the petition had the support of Pfaff, who joined the proceedings virtually.Singhvi had said the fact that every head of state from India, over decades, had paid obeisance at Renko-Ji temple was proof that the freedom fighter's ashes were there.The CJI-led bench had said there was no doubt that he was one of the country's greatest freedom fighters, but refused to entertain Ray's plea.Justice Bagchi had said, "We respect her (Pfaff's) sentiments and we will ensure that her sentiments are translated into legal action. But she must step forward."Singhvi had withdrawn the petition. Heeding SC's suggestion, Pfaff filed the petition seeking repatriation of Netaji's ashes by India or, as an alternative, "facilitate her to repatriate the ashes".
Article Summary: Netaji subhas chandra bose’s octogenarian germany-based daughter, anita pfaff, moved supreme court on tuesday. Pfaff's petition complained of "india's prolonged failure to take a final, reasoned and time-bound decision" to repatriate to india the "mortal remains/ashes attributed to netaji"

Article Title: SC seeks reply from govt for 'not utilising' Rs 1,000cr green fund
Article Text:
SC asked the Centre and CPCB to provide details of how the over Rs 1,000-crore Environment Protection Fund was used

NEW DELHI: Supreme Court Tuesday asked the Centre and Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) to provide details of how the over Rs 1,000-crore Environment Protection Fund (EPF), which was set up in 2008 to compensate people affected by hazardous substances, was utilised.Expressing surprise at a PIL by Gyan Prakash alleging that no compensation had been paid from the fund, a bench headed by CJI Surya Kant also asked govt and CPCB to explain how it proposes to use the corpus built over the years.Instituted under Public Liability Act, 1991, which was enacted following the Bhopal gas tragedy , the primary purpose of EPF is to offer immediate relief to victims and those injured in accidents involving hazardous substances. Prakash said there had been many incidents of oil tankers catching fire, but those who lost their lives or were injured did not get anything from the fund.The 35-year-old law requires industries producing or handling hazardous material to mandatorily obtain public liability insurance. Apart from contributions from these industries, EPF gets funds from compensation awarded by National Green Tribunal for damage caused to the environment. The court was informed that the fund has been managed by CPCB from 2024.
Article Summary: Sc asked the centre and cpcb to provide details of how the over rs 1,000-crore environment protection fund was used. A pil by gyan prakash alleging that no compensation had been paid from the fund. The fund was set up in 2008 to compensate people affected by hazardous substances.

Article Title: NEET protest: TVK slams govt; Rahul, Priyanka released from detention — top developments
Article Text:
The developments came as thousands of protesters returned to Jantar Mantar to continue the agitation.

FIR filed as video shows RAF jawan assaulted

TVK condemns Rahul's detention, seeks NEET abolition



Rahul, Priyanka released after detention



Pradhan hits out at Congress



Police detail injuries, register six FIRs



Sonam Wangchuk continues fast



NEW DELHI: Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra were released after being detained by Delhi Police during a protest near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence. The party had marched to Lok Kalyan Marg demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged paper leak and the police action against student protesters.The protests also drew support from several opposition leaders, while Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) strongly criticised the Centre over Rahul Gandhi's detention and renewed its demand for the abolition of NEET. Meanwhile, the BJP accused the Congress of politicising the issue and defended the police action, saying the protest near the Prime Minister's residence was held without permission.The developments came as thousands of protesters returned to Jantar Mantar to continue the agitation.Delhi Police registered an FIR after a Rapid Action Force (RAF) jawan was allegedly chased and assaulted by a group of protesters during the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) "Sansad Chalo" march in New Delhi.A video of the incident, widely shared on social media, appears to show the jawan being surrounded, pursued and beaten during Monday's protest. Police said an investigation is under way to identify those responsible.Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) condemned the detention of Rahul Gandhi and criticised the police action against opposition leaders."The crushing of democracy in the capital is strongly condemnable! The arrest of Lok Sabha Opposition Leader Mr. Rahul Gandhi, who joined the field in support of the youth's non-violent protest held in Delhi, by being dragged by the Delhi Police and carried away in handcuffs, is a blatant attack on democratic rights. This is strongly condemnable," the party said.TVK also renewed its opposition to the NEET examination."From the moment the NEET exam was implemented, not only has its credibility been lacking, but students continue to face mental stress, with their future dreams turning into a massive question mark. The harm caused to students every year due to the NEET exam shows no sign of stopping. Therefore, we strongly demand that the Union government completely abolish the NEET exam," it said.Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and several opposition leaders were detained after Congress workers marched towards the Prime Minister's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg.Rahul Gandhi, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and other leaders were taken to Chhatrasal Stadium before being released later in the day. Priyanka Gandhi was taken to Mandir Marg Police Station, where Congress Parliamentary Party chairperson Sonia Gandhi later arrived.Congress leaders said they protested because they were not allowed to raise the NEET issue in Parliament. Rahul Gandhi said they marched to the Prime Minister's residence "to demand answers from him for the brutalities against young students yesterday" and demanded the resignation of the Prime Minister and the Home Minister.The protest was joined by Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, KC Venugopal, Pawan Khera, Karnataka chief minister DK Shivakumar, Kerala Leader of Opposition VD Satheesan, Supriya Sule and several other opposition leaders.Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan accused the Congress of using students for political purposes."LoP Shri @RahulGandhi and @INCIndia continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament," he wrote on X.He said the government was ready for a discussion on NEET in Parliament but alleged that the Congress preferred "political spectacle over democratic debate"."We owe our students more than outrage. We owe them answers, reforms and accountability. That is exactly what our government remains committed to delivering," Pradhan said.BJP MP Sambit Patra also criticised the protest, saying demonstrations should be held only at designated locations. He said the Prime Minister's residence is a high-security zone and staging a protest there without permission was "inappropriate".Delhi Police said six FIRs have been registered in connection with the violence during Monday's Parliament march. Two FIRs were registered at Parliament Street police station, while one each was registered at Connaught Place, Tilak Marg, Barakhamba Road and Kartavya Path police stations.DCP Northeast Delhi Rahul Alawal said six police personnel were injured during the violence. He said Constable Mohit suffered serious injuries to his head, below his eyes and on his legs and remains on medical rest. Additional DCP Sandeep Lamba, Inspector Rampal Goswami and some other officers have resumed duty despite being advised to rest.Station House Officer of Connaught Place police station Sukhbir Singh Malik said he was assaulted when a section of protesters allegedly turned violent near Regal Cinema. Malik said his helmet prevented a head injury, but he suffered injuries to his elbows, calf muscle and waist after being dragged to the ground. The police said several other personnel, including an ACP, an Inspector, a Head Constable and a driver, were also injured.Activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his indefinite hunger strike after being shifted from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram following directions from the Delhi High Court.Sources told news agency ANI said Union Health Minister JP Nadda and Minister of State in the Prime Minister's Office Jitendra Singh visited Medanta Hospital to enquire about Wangchuk's health.The Delhi High Court directed Medanta Hospital to form a specialised medical team to supervise Wangchuk's treatment while allowing him to continue his fast under constant medical supervision. His wife, Gitanjali Angmo, said he would continue the hunger strike in solidarity with students allegedly injured during the police action.
Article Summary: Congress leaders rahul gandhi and priyanka gandhi vadra released after being detained by delhi police. The developments came as thousands of protesters returned to jantar mantar to continue the agitation. Tamilaga vettri kazhagam (tvk) strongly criticised the centre over rahul gandhi's detention and renewed its demand for the abolition of neet.

Article Title: Opposition using protest to find political relevance: BJP
Article Text:
A day after the Chalo Sansad march

NEW DELHI: BJP had a go at opposition parties Tuesday as Aam Aadmi Party attacked Congress over its dharna outside PM's residence on the NEET-UG paper leak issue, saying it is quite obvious that the "sponsored" protests were never about students but about the opposition's search for political relevance."It is quite obvious that the sponsored protests were never about students. No affected student was given a platform. Opposition politicians frequented Jantar Mantar in search of political relevance, and now, we see Congress and AAP squabbling over credit," BJP said as the Arvind Kejriwal-led party accused Congress of trying to weaken Cockroach Janta Party's protest.Keeping up its attack over the protests, BJP alleged that all political assets of those resenting India's rise have been activated. "But India's democracy is neither so fragile nor are the people of this country so naive that they cannot see their mandate being held to ransom by the sponsored outrage of a few," BJP's Amit Malviya said, adding the timing is no coincidence.As India accelerates towards becoming a developed nation, every transformational initiative is met with a familiar cycle of disruption, disinformation and manufactured outrage, he claimed.Malviya said at a time when the country is making rapid strides in different sectors, those who failed to stop India's rise electorally now seek to slow it through perpetual agitation, manufactured crises and a relentless campaign to erode public confidence.
Article Summary: Aam aadmi party attacked congress over its dharna outside pm's residence on the neet-ug paper leak issue. Bjp said it is quite obvious that the "sponsored" protests were never about students but about the opposition's search for political relevance.

Article Title: 'Nadir of Indians' integrity': Allahabad HC judge slams Ram Mandir theft, backs death penalty
Article Text:
Allahabad High Court on Ram Temple theft (ANI image on right)

Backs death penalty

Supreme Court orders fresh SIT probe

The case so far

NEW DELHI: Allahabad High Court judge, on Tuesday, made strong observations on the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple in Ayodhya, saying the incident reflected the"nadir of Indians' integrity"and that those unfazed by it could not be shamed.The observations were made while the bench was hearing a matter related to the Uttar Pradesh government's recent bulldozer actions. During the hearing, Justice Sreedharan criticised the deep-rooted corruption in public institutions.The judge also advocated the death penalty for offences under the Prevention of Corruption Act.Justice Atul Sreedharan said, "The recent controversy relating to the theft of donations at the Ram temple is the proverbial last straw on the camel's back. Nothing can shame people who remain unfazed by the theft at the Ram temple which epitomises the nadir of Indians' integrity".""The average Indian has accepted corruption as normal. He does not consider it wrong unless someone is caught. India ranks 91st out of 182 countries on the Transparency list, and even that does not make us feel ashamed," he added.The Supreme Court earlier has directed the constitution of a fresh Special Investigation Team (SIT) to probe the alleged Ram Mandir donation theft case.A bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant said the new SIT should be led by a senior IPS officer to ensure a fair and conclusive investigation."We only want to issue a word of caution. Do not politicise this matter. At first glance, this appears to be an offence under criminal law. It must be investigated fairly, independently and with complete honesty. The investigation should be taken to its logical conclusion," the CJI observed.The controversy erupted on June 7 after former Samajwadi Party minister Pawan Pandey alleged that donations worth Rs 5 crore to Rs 7.5 crore had been stolen from the Ram Mandir.Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav questioned the government's silence and sought a judicial probe, while Ram Mandir Trust general secretary Champat Rai rejected the allegations. BJP leader Dr Rajneesh Singh later demanded a CBI investigation, following which the Prime Minister's Office sought a report from the temple trust.An FIR was registered on June 25 on the complaint of trustee Krishna Mohan. Police has subsequently arrested eight accused in the matter till now.
Article Summary: Allahabad high court judge criticises deep-rooted corruption in public institutions. Justice atul sreedharan advocates death penalty for offences under prevention of corruption act. Supreme court orders constitution of a fresh special investigation team (sit) to probe the alleged ram mandir donation theft case.

Article Title: 'Picked up in car, sexually assaulted, filmed': Bengal woman 'gang raped' at Karnataka hotel; 3 arrested
Article Text:
Mysuru police have detained three youths from T Narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape (Representative image).

(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)

MYSURU: Mysuru district police arrested three youths from T Narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape of a woman in a lodge room in Kollegala, Chamarajanagar district, last week.The woman, stated to be from West Bengal, has been moved to a rehabilitation centre for counselling and support, and her identity is being verified.Superintendent of police Mallikarjun Baladandi said a gang-rape case has been registered under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) based on the survivor’s complaint, and the three accused—Rihan, Kiran and Kishore—have been arrested.According to the complaint, the accused picked her up in a car, took her to a hotel in the neighbouring district, sexually assaulted her in hotel room and then attempted to extort money from her.She reportedly escaped after promising to pay them money kept at home. She got out of the car on reaching her residence, locked herself inside and raised an alarm, after which neighbours alerted police.Police, who are doubting the veracity of the complaint, said preliminary inquiry indicates the woman voluntarily travelled with the accused to the hotel. Investigators also claim a dispute arose after the accused found she had filmed a private act on her phone. The accused allegedly seized her mobile phone and formatted the phone and then demanded Rs 1 lakh from her.Though incident of rape is said to have happened in Chamarajanagar limits, Mysuru police have registered a case as the woman was allegedly picked up from a village in T Narasipura.
Article Summary: Mysuru police have detained three youths from t narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape. The woman, stated to be from west bengal, has been moved to a rehabilitation centre for counselling and support. The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per supreme court directives.

Article Title: 'Government had no interest in debate': Rahul Gandhi on why Congress protested outside PM's residence
Article Text:
Rahul issued a video message explaining why they staged a protest, demands debate on paper leak in Parliament

NEW DELHI: The leader of opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi issued statement addressing the students and Prime Minister Narendra Modi after the Congress party's protest outside PM residence over the NEET paper leak.He shared a video message on X, saying it was his "direct message to the students of India and the Prime Minister." He backed the students and demanded a proper discussion in Lok Sabha on Wednesday during the ongoing Monsoon Session of the Parliament"This morning I met the Speaker of the Lok Sabha and told him that it was important that we be allowed to debate what happened to the students. The previous day, the Speaker had said he needed to take permission from the government. But it became very clear to us in the hours that followed that the government had no interest in holding a debate," he said.Continuing his allegations, he added, "So, we took a decision, that we will stage a protest in front of Prime Minister's house and get the students' issues to national attention"Further questioning the govt, he added, "Why is the Indian education system collapsing? Why are papers leaking? Why is education so expensive in India? Why do families have to financially ruin themselves to educate their children? These are legitimate questions, and there is absolutely nothing wrong with asking them."Rahul also alleged that cases were being filed against students and asserted that the entire Opposition stood with them. Reiterating the demands he said, "The students' demands are, firstly, the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan and HM Amit Shah. Secondly, action should be taken against those who have beaten and humiliated the students, and all the cases against the students should be withdrawn. Finally, the entire opposition wants that there should be a debate on this tomorrow in the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha on this issue. .."Further addressing PM Modi he said, "The entire Opposition stands with the students. We want reforms in the examination and education systems. We will continue to fight for your voice and your future."This comes after Rahul along with Priyanka Gandh iwere detained for protesting outside PM residence. They were later released. The Congress leaders tied plastic bands around their hands and raised slogans, demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak.Read more: How Congress surprised its own MPs, govt with protest outside PM's residence
Article Summary: Rahul issued a video message explaining why they staged a protest, demands debate on paper leak in parliament. He also alleged that cases were being filed against students and asserted that the entire opposition stood with them. This comes after rahul along with priyanka gandh iwere detained for protesting outside pm residence.

Article Title: Health minister JP Nadda, MoS Jitendra Singh meet Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital: Report
Article Text:
JP Nadda and minister of state for the Prime Minister's Office, Dr Jitendra Singh met Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta hospital

NEW DELHI: Union minister Jagat Prakash Nadda, minister of state for the Prime Minister's office Dr Jitendra Singh went to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram and met Activist Sonam Wangchuk to enquire about his health, as per PTI sources.This came after the Delhi high court on Tuesday ordered the immediate transfer of scientist and activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram amid his prolonged hunger strike over the alleged NEET paper leak.The division bench directed that Wangchuk be treated by doctors at Medanta as per established medical protocol and said he must follow the medical advice of the treating team after his transfer.The order came after Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali Angmo, challenged a single judge's decision refusing permission to shift him from Safdarjung Hospital.During the hearing, the Centre informed the high court that it had "no objection" to Wangchuk being shifted to Medanta Hospital. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, however, urged that Wangchuk "remain under continuous medical supervision" and "not be discharged except on the advice of Medanta's doctors."Wangchuk entered the 24th day of his hunger strike on Tuesday as the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) stepped up its protest against the Centre over the alleged NEET paper leak and renewed its demand for Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Protesters also continued their sit-in at Delhi's Jantar Mantar.
Article Summary: Jp nadda and minister of state for the prime minister's office, dr jitendra singh met sonam wangchuk at medanta hospital. This came after the delhi high court on tuesday ordered the immediate transfer of scientist and activist wangchuk from safdarjung hospital to medanta hospital in gurugram.

Article Title: Omar Abdullah ‘flees’ Jantar Mantar in auto, says CJP supporters followed him
Article Text:
Omar Abdullah travelled in the auto-rickshaw along with his two sons, Zahir Abdullah and Zamir Abdullah. Photo credit: X @OmarAbdullah

SRINAGAR: Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah Tuesday said his now-viral autorickshaw ride after Monday’s protest in New Delhi for restoration of statehood was a sudden decision prompted by concerns after supporters of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) started following him.Videos of Omar sitting inside a green-and-yellow autorickshaw, carrying protest placards after the demonstration, have spread all across social media. “It was completely spontaneous,” he said, talking to reporters after a presser.“I started walking back after the protest when some of my party workers joined me. Then the Cockroach Party people also began following us,” Omar said. “I noticed the Delhi Police was getting very jumpy, with more police vehicles arriving. I thought these Cockroach Party supporters might end up being tear-gassed as people would assume I was leading them towards Rajpath and Parliament. I didn’t want that on my head,” he said. “Then I saw an autorickshaw, jumped in and left.”Earlier, addressing a press conference, Omar justified the protest for statehood in New Delhi but excluded restoration of Article 370 from it, saying the incumbent govt at the Centre, which has revoked the special status of J&K, will not restore it.“I have always told the people of Jammu & Kashmir that expecting the same people who took Article 370 away to hand it back to us on a platter is unrealistic,” he said. “We have to work to restore J&K’s special status, but we are not going to get it under this govt.”Omar said restoration of statehood is the first step towards any discussion on restoring special status for J&K, arguing that special status has little meaning without a state’s legislative powers. “What does Article 370 mean without a state? What is the meaning of Article 371 without a state?” he said.Asked about the absence of senior Congress leaders like Rahul Gandhi during the protest, Omar said the circumstances in New Delhi made it difficult for many opposition leaders to participate. “Those of us who were affected by the tear gas know what the situation was like.”He said postponing the protest was not a practical option despite the disruption.About his US president Donald Trump remarks on J&K statehood, he said, “I have never internationalized Kashmir. Did I ask Mr Trump to get our statehood?”
Article Summary: Omar abdullah travelled in the auto-rickshaw along with his two sons, zahir abdullah and zamir abdullah. Videos of omar sitting inside a green-and-yellow autorickshaw, carrying protest placards after the demonstration, have spread all across social media.

Article Title: Modi government picks a Sikh again to head minorities panel
Article Text:
Harjit Singh Grewal is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt after Iqbal Singh Lalpura

NEW DELHI: After remaining without a chief for 15 months, National Commission for Minorities (NCM) Tuesday saw its new chairman Harjit Singh Grewal, assume charge. He is the second Sikh to head the body under the Modi govt after Iqbal Singh Lalpura. The post had been vacant since April 2025, when Lalpura's three-year term ended.Before Lalpura and Grewal, the NCM had largely been headed by members of the Muslim community since its inception in 1993, except between 2003 and 2006, when Tarlochan Singh was chairperson. Syed Ghayorul Hasan Rizvi was NCM chief from May 2017 to 2020. He was preceded by Naseem Ahmad (2014-2017).Grewal's appointment gains significance as it comes ahead of 2027 Punjab assembly polls. A senior BJP functionary and national executive committee member from Punjab, Grewal is regarded as one of the most vocal Sikh voices in the state.A notification on Grewal's appointment was issued by ministry of minority affairs on July 16. He will serve a three-year term from the date he assumed office, or until further orders, whichever is earlier.The ministry said his appointment marked "the beginning of a renewed commitment towards promoting the welfare, inclusion, empowerment and socio-economic development of minority communities."Muslim representative S. Munawari Begum took charge as NCM vice-chairperson, while Glenn Souza Ticlo joined as the Christian representative. Parsi representative Berjis Desai joined the commission in March.The NCM now has a chairperson and three members, with three posts still vacant.
Article Summary: Harjit singh grewal is the second sikh to head the body under the modi govt. The post had been vacant since april 2025, when iqbal singh lalpura's three-year term ended. The ncm had largely been headed by members of the muslim community.

Article Title: 'We owe students more than outrage': Education minister Pradhan slams Cong protest
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan on Tuesday accused leader of opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi and Congress of "shamelessly exploiting students as political tools" following the grand-old-party's protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence over the NEET paper leak.In a social media post on X, Pradhan said Congress chose to stage a dharna outside PM Modi's residence, causing inconvenience to the public and disregarding established security protocols."LoP Shri Rahul Gandhi and Congress continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament ," the education minister said."Shri Rahul Gandhi and Congress chose to stage a dharna outside the Hon'ble Prime Minister's residence, causing inconvenience to the public and disregarding established security protocols," he added.Further, Pradhan said Congress "chose spectacle over democratic debate" despite the government conveying its readiness for a comprehensive discussion on the NEET paper leak issue."Even after the government conveyed its readiness for a comprehensive discussion, the Congress chose political spectacle over democratic debate. Their objective was never solutions for students; it was disruption for political headlines," he said."For Rahul Gandhi, this is not about students. This is about manufacturing confrontation after every genuine avenue for discussion has been opened," he added.Pradhan said the government owes students answers, reforms and accountability rather than outrage."Our government remains 100% committed to discussing NEET and addressing every genuine concern of our youth on the floor of the House. The students of India deserve far better than being treated as props in a political campaign. They deserve certainty over chaos, solutions over slogans and responsibility over disruption," the education minister said."We owe our students more than outrage. We owe them answers, reforms and accountability. That is exactly what our government remains committed to delivering," he added.
Article Summary: Dharmendra pradhan accused rahul gandhi and congress of "shamelessly exploiting students as political tools" he said congress chose to stage a dharna outside pm modi's residence, causing inconvenience to the public and disregarding established security protocols.

Article Title: 'Ghor paap': PM Modi on NEET paper leak; vows strict action against culprits
Article Text:
PM Modi, according to Rijiju, also said that "security and future of youth" is the government's priority.

The Monday mayhem

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi termed the NEET paper leak issue "ghor paap (grave sin)" and assured strict action against the culprits who "play with the future of youth". The reaction came during the NDA's parliamentary party meeting and was conveyed by Union minister Kiren Rijiju Rijiju, who is the minister of parliamentary affairs, said the prime minister stated that "NEET paper leak should not be an issue of partisan politics" as it is a "national issue and not a matter of one state or the Centre alone".PM Modi, according to Rijiju, also said that "security and future of youth" is the government's priority.The Prime Minister's reaction comes in the backdrop of protest led by youth and Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) members, who were protesting against the perpetual paper leaks, and alleged irregularities in the education system.The uproar over the NEET leak and lack of accountability at the senior-most level exploded in the Capital on Monday as protestors, mostly students and youths, clashed with police -- leading the cops to resort to lathi charge, firing of tear gas to disperse the protesting crowd marching towards the Parliament.The protesters had three main demands: Resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, discharging Sonam Wangchuk from hospital and providing compensation to the kin of NEET aspirants who died by suicide after the leak forced the government to re-examination.More than 170 people, including Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said.According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC). Many of those brought to both hospitals were discharged after receiving first aid.Officials at RML said that by 7 pm, the hospital's surgery department had registered 65 medico-legal cases (MLCs) involving both protesters and police personnel. They added that detailed information on all cases was still being compiled.Hospital authorities also said around 10 MLCs involved people who had been on hunger strike. All of them were reported to be in stable condition.Sources at RML said nearly 40 of the injured brought to the hospital were police personnel, while the remaining patients were protesters. Around 35 people were admitted, with the rest treated and discharged. Between seven and eight patients required treatment in the hospital's red zone because of the seriousness of their injuries.Among those admitted was a 20-year-old woman who suffered a hypoxic brain injury, a condition caused when the brain is deprived of adequate oxygen following severe trauma, hospital sources said.The exact circumstances in which she sustained her injuries remain unclear. According to hospital sources, she may have been injured during the clashes or after falling in the stampede that followed, but the sequence of events has not yet been established.Doctors said most of the remaining patients suffered bruises, baton injuries, head wounds and facial injuries, including split lips, forehead wounds and scalp lacerations. Although many had visible injuries to the head and face, no fractures were reported among patients treated at RML.At Lady Hardinge Medical College, at least 24 injured people were treated. Most were discharged after first aid, while those who arrived later in the evening continued to undergo medical evaluation. Doctors at the hospital said none of the patients brought there had sustained major injuries.
Article Summary: Pm narendra modi termed the neet paper leak issue "ghor paap (grave sin)" and assured strict action against culprits. Pm modi, according to rijiju, also said that "security and future of youth" is the government's priority. The reaction comes in the backdrop of protest led by youth and cockroach janta party members.

Article Title: CJP back at Jantar Mantar, cops break Congress protest at PM house
Article Text:
Rahul detained by cops; later released Govt: Ready To Discuss NEET Issue In Parl Rahul detained by cops; later released Govt: Ready To Discuss NEET Issue In Parl Rahul detained by cops; later released 1

2

NEW DELHI: Stepping up their protest over NEET paper leak, senior Congress functionaries, including party chief Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi staged a surprise sit-in outside PM Modi's residence, as govt and BJP accused Rahul of precipitating confrontation with his protest in a high-security zone which showed he is a "symbol of anarchy".With Rahul insisting on education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation after govt had agreed to a debate in Parliament on the paper leak and giving a call to "every patriotic Indian" to join the protest, police forcibly removed the Congress functionaries, including Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, and Samajwadi Party's Akhilesh Yadav, who had joined the sit-in.Police said, to evade suspicion from police and intelligence agencies, the organisers allegedly used an elderly leader's birthday as cover, inviting politicians, supporters and media for a "cake-cutting ceremony" at an adjacent govt bungalow. They then went to the residence's restricted exit gate, according to police.Rahul Gandhi was taken to Chhatrasal stadium and Priyanka Gandhi to Mandir Marg police station. Later, they were released along with the others, police said."Rahul's behaviour demonstrates that he does not take the dignity of the leader of opposition's office seriously. We have consistently maintained that he is emerging as a symbol of anarchy," BJP chief Nitin Nabin said.Videos of Rahul resisting detention lying on the ground went viral. "Democracy in India is happening right now," he said, as he was pulled away. "An attack on students is an attack on every Indian family. PM Modi believes he can get away without answers, without consequences. He cannot. Not this time," he said.Govt had sent Union minister Jitendra Singh and home secretary Govind Mohan to speak with Rahul to lift the protest, but in vain. Singh later accused Rahul of conduct unbecoming of LoP in Lok Sabha after his refusal to lift the sit-in, despite govt agreeing to his sole demand of having a debate on NEET-UG paper leak issue in Parliament.Singh said Rahul's demand for Pradhan's resignation was an afterthought after the top govt leadership agreed to his initial solitary demand for a debate in Parliament over the paper leak and related issues, including protests. "For a leader like him, who is also LoP, to go back on his word is contrary to every norm of democracy," he said.Nabin said Rahul and other protestors' behaviour and their attempt to "incite anarchy" outside Modi's residence showed this is not politics of dissent, but politics of anarchy. "It is particularly concerning that a senior leader like Mallikarjun Kharge accompanied Rahul," he added.The dharna started around 3.30pm and went on for over three hours, and also included two Congress CMs V D Satheesan (Kerala) and D K Shivakumar (Karnataka).Congress' first-ever protest led by its Rae Bareli MP Rahul on the issue in the national capital is being seen in political circles as the party's attempt to upstage its 'secular' rivals by associating itself with the angst of a section of the youth without conceding leadership to Cockroach Janta Party.Taken aback by Congress's protest, AAP's former Delhi CM Atishi accused Congress of indulging in a 'jugalbandi' with govt to weaken CJP's stir. "Modi ji got Rahul Gandhi to stage a protest outside his own residence," AAP MP Sanjay Singh alleged.
Article Summary: Congress' first-ever protest led by its rae bareli mp rahul on the issue in the national capital is being seen in political circles as the party's attempt to upstage its'secular' rivals. Police forcibly removed the congress functionaries, including priyanka gandhi vadra, and samajwadi party's akhilesh yadav, who had joined the sit-in.

Article Title: No legal aid to accused, SC orders fresh trial in 30-year-old blast case
Article Text:
SC set aside conviction and death sentence of an accused in Rajasthan’s 1996 Samleti bus blast case

NEW DELHI: Holding that legitimacy of a judicial verdict is measured not by severity of the punishment but by the fairness of the processes through which guilt is determined, Supreme Court Tuesday set aside the conviction and death sentence of an accused in Rajasthan's 1996 Samleti bus blast case in which 14 people had died, noting that meaningful legal assistance was not made available to him to defend himself.To find out if the accused, Abdul Hameed, was represented by a lawyer, SC interacted with him through video from jail and analysed the order sheet of the trial court. It concluded that the accused remained undefended in court proceedings.A bench of Justices Vikram Nath, Sanjay Karol and Sandeep Mehta directed a fresh trial be conducted in the three-decade-old case."The right to effective legal representation, the presumption of innocence, the burden upon the prosecution to establish guilt beyond reasonable doubt and the requirement that evidence be scrutinised with objectivity and caution are not mere procedural formalities. They are substantive guarantees that distinguish the rule of law from the rule of public opinion," the court observed."Justice must not only be done but must manifestly be seen to be done. The legitimacy of a judicial verdict rests not merely upon the outcome reached, but equally upon the fairness, transparency and integrity of the process by which that outcome is achieved," it added.The court admitted that acts of violence directed against innocent civilians strike at the very foundations of social order and generate understandable feelings of fear, grief and outrage but said it was precisely in such cases that courts must remain steadfast in their adherence to constitutional values."The gravity of the allegation cannot be permitted to dilute the standard of proof, nor can the enormity of the crime justify a departure from settled principles governing criminal adjudication. The greater the public outcry, the greater becomes the responsibility of the court to ensure that the determination of guilt is based solely upon law and evidence," it said.The court requested acting Chief Justice of Rajasthan HC to designate a special court in Jaipur to conduct the de novo trial (retrial). It said the special court shall be presided over by an officer of the Rajasthan higher judicial service having not less than seven years' experience of conducting sessions trials and it shall make every endeavour to conclude the trial within a year from the date of assignment of the case.It said if the accused is unable to engage a counsel, the special court shall secure competent legal representation for him to ensure effective and meaningful legal assistance throughout the proceedings.
Article Summary: Sc set aside conviction and death sentence of an accused in rajasthan’s 1996 samleti bus blast case. Justices vikram nath, sanjay karol and sandeep mehta directed a fresh trial be conducted. To find out if the accused, abdul hameed, was represented by a lawyer, sc interacted with him through video from jail.

Article Title: Another SC judge, the 3rd, opts out of hearing Gadling bail plea
Article Text: New Delhi: Supreme Court judge Shree Chandrashekhar on Tuesday recused himself from hearing the bail plea of lawyer-activist Surendra Gadling in the 2016 Gadchiroli arson case. He is the third judge to recuse himself in the case after Justice Atul Chandurkar and Justice MM Sundresh, reports Amit Anand Choudhary.At the outset of the hearing, which was listed before a bench of Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and Shree Chandrashekhar, the court asked for the matter to be listed before another bench."It will go to some other combination; my brother has some difficulty. Post before the bench in which one of us isn't there," Justice Mishra said.Gadling had approached the apex court challenging the Bombay high court order denying him bail in the arson case. He has been booked under various sections of UAPA and IPC for allegedly being part of a conspiracy by Maoists to set fire to more than 80 vehicles transporting iron ore from Surjagarh mines in Maharashtra's Gadchiroli.
Article Summary: Supreme court judge shree chandrashekhar recused himself from hearing the bail plea of lawyer-activist surendra gadling. He is the third judge to recuse himself in the case after justice atul chandurkar and justice mm sundresh, reports amit anand choudhary.

Article Title: 'Went where they least expected us': Priyanka Gandhi on 'Chalo PM House' protest
Article Text:
'Marched to PM Modi's house to demand answers'

'Join our dharna'

Union minister Jitendra Singh meets Rahul

Jitendra Singh claims Rahul changed demands mid-negotiation

Police remove Rahul from protest site

'Modi Ji, try every pressure'

Sonia reaches police station after Rahul, Priyanka detained

'Went where they least expected us'

'Wah! kya jugalbandi hai'

Opposition leaders joins protest

Rahul, Priyanka meet admitted students

Pradhan slams

Congress protest

outside PM residence

NEW DELHI: Senior Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi , Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, on Tuesday took their protest to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg, alleging police excesses against students during the Cockroach Janata Party's march to Parliament.Congress MPs and senior leaders raised slogans against PM Narendra Modi and the Bharatiya Janata Party over the NEET paper leak and demanded the resignation of PM Narendra Modi and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.In a social media post on X, Rahul said that he marched to PM Modi's residence to demand answers for the brutalities against students on Monday."We have marched to PM Modi’s house to demand answers from him for the brutalities against young students yesterday. The government doesn’t want to take any accountability or does it want to have a debate on it in Parliament," Rahul said."PM and HM must resign for destroying the future of India’s youth," he added.In another social media post on X, Rahul called on "patriotic Indians" to join the protest outside the PM's residence."An attack on students is an attack on every Indian family. PM Modi believes he can get away without answers, without consequences.He cannot. Not this time," Rahul said."I ask every patriotic Indian who believes our students deserve justice - join us in dharna in front of the Prime Minister’s residence. The voice of India’s students will not be ignored," he added.Nearly 20 minutes into the protest, minister of state in the Prime Minister's Office Jitendra Singh met Rahul at the protest site and urged him to end the dharna.After holding talks with Rahul, Jitendra Singh accused the LoP of backtracking from his earlier demands and refusing to end the dharna.In a social media post on X, Singh said, "Some time ago, it came to light that the Congress Party's national president, Shri Mallikarjun Kharge, and other senior leaders with him, such as Shri Rahul Gandhi, Smt. Priyanka Gandhi, and others, suddenly sat down for a sit-in protest near Akbar Road with their supporters. In view of the dignity of this senior delegation, the government sent me personally, along with the Union home secretary, Shri Govind Mohan, to the spot to hold talks with these senior leaders. A request was made to Shri Rahul Gandhi to end his sit-in, as this site is not meant for protests, and holding a sit-in here is causing considerable inconvenience to the general public."Also Read| 'Rahul wanted a confrontation': Jitendra Singh details fallout of talks with Congress leader "Shri Rahul Gandhi put forward the demand that if the government agreed to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement, he would immediately end his sit-in. Within a few moments, after taking permission from the government's top leadership, Shri Rahul Gandhi was assured that his demand had been accepted, and the government was ready to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement," he added.Singh further said that after the government's message was conveyed to Rahul, he also sought assurance on the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan."When this information was conveyed to Shri Rahul Gandhi, he said that he would not agree to this alone but also sought assurance on the resignation of the Union education minister. He said that now I have two demands: discussion in Parliament and the resignation of the Union education minister. When he was reminded that earlier he had only demanded discussion, he said that now my demands have changed. When an attempt was made to humbly explain to him that for a senior leader like him to backtrack on his word so quickly does not befit him, he said that it is his prerogative. When the Home secretary tried to explain that this site is not suitable for a sit-in, his response was that it is also his prerogative to sit wherever he wants," the Union minister said."For a senior leader like Rahul Gandhi and the leader of the opposition to go back on his word is unfortunate and contrary to every norm of democracy. The government is ready to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement. In contrast, the Congress Party has not given any notice to date for a formal discussion on this serious matter as per the rules. Shri Rahul Gandhi's behavior does not align with the principles of democracy," he added.Meanwhile Delhi Police tried to forcibly remove Rahul, Priyanka and Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav from the protest site.While being detained by the Delhi Police, Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra said, "They are cowards. They are scared. We are not scared of them. They are scared of us."Additionally, police also detained several senior Congress leaders, including Charanjit Singh Channi, MP K Suresh, and Kanhaiya Kumar."This is BJP's terrorism. They don't listen to the students," Channi said as he was being taken away by the Delhi Police.Congress MP D K Suresh said that the police were "brutally detaining Congress workers.""Brutally, they are detaining us. We are protesting in front of the Prime Minister's residence because our students were brutally attacked yesterday by the Delhi Police. The Prime Minister is not saying anything, the home minister is not making any statement," he said.After he was detained, Rahul again took to X and said that fight against students will not stopped."Modi Ji, try every pressure, exert all your might - this fight for justice for students will now not stop or be halted."After detaining Rahul and Priyanka from the protest site, the Delhi Police took the leaders to Chhatrasal Stadium Police Station and Mandir Marg Police Station."Leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi has been detained at Chhatrasal Stadium. And General Secretary Priyanka Gandhi ji has been brought to Mandir Marg Police Station. If this is the cost to be paid for fighting for the youth -- so be it," Congress leader Supriya Shrinate said in a post on X.Later, veteran Congress leader Sonia Gandhi arrived at Mandir Marg Police Station. Following her arrival, a huge crowd gathered outside the police station.Released after the detention, Priyanka said that students should be heard over alleged irregularities and a discussion should be held in Parliament."If the leader of the opposition wishes to speak in Parliament, he is not allowed to do so. If we seek permission to stage a protest, that permission is denied. So we went to a place where they least expected us," Priyanka said."What is the problem in listening to the children of our country, the future of our nation? They have a genuine demand. As their representatives, it is our responsibility to raise and empower their voices. In a democracy, protesting is a fundamental right. We will raise our voice wherever it is required. Our demand is clear and remains the same from the beginning. We have to fix the broken education system. Dharmendra Pradhan has to resign. A discussion should be held in Parliament. And the students should be heard," she added.Meanwhile, former Delhi chief minister Atishi took a jibe at Congress' protest and said that CJP and Sonam Wangchuk were protesting at Jantar Mantar for the past one month but the government refused to talk to them, but initiated dialogue with Rahul Gandhi within one hour of his protest."This is amazing! CJP and Sonam Wangchuk: One month protest at Jantar Mantar. Modi govt refuses to talk to them," Atishi said in a social media post on X."Rahul Gandhi: Allowed to protest outside PM residence. In one hour Modi Govt starts dialogue. Wah! Kya jugalbandi hai!" she added.Meanwhile, opposition leaders, including Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule, also joined the protest in support of the Congress' demand for accountability over the alleged police action against students.Several MPs from INDIA bloc parties were also seen at the protest site, with the opposition presenting a united front as it sought to mount pressure on the government over the handling of the students' agitation and examination irregularities.Earlier in the day, Rahul and Priyanka met students admitted to the Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital after they were injured in police action during the CJP-led protest.They also met the mother of an injured young woman protester who is on ventilator support at the RML Hospital, news agency PTI reported, citing sources.In a post on X, the Congress said Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi, along with General Secretary (Organisation) K C Venugopal, met the students injured during the "police brutality and lathi charge" in Monday's youth protest."Today, the future of students in the country is being trampled, and lathis are being rained down on those who raise their voices, for which Narendra Modi should apologise. The students' demand is absolutely clear -- the government should take responsibility for the paper leak and Dharmendra Pradhan should resign," the party said.This comes a day after violent clashes erupted in Delhi as thousands of protesters from the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) and students marched from Jantar Mantar towards Parliament, demanding the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan over recurring examination irregularities, including the NEET-UG paper leaks.Pradhan, in his statement, accused Rahul Gandhi and Congress of "shamelessly exploiting students as political tools" following the grand-old-party's protest outside PM Modi's residence over the NEET paper leak.In a social media post on X, Pradhan said Congress chose to stage a dharna outside PM Modi's residence, causing inconvenience to the public and disregarding established security protocols."LoP Shri Rahul Gandhi and Congress continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament," the education minister said.Read more: Education minister Pradhan slams Cong protest
Article Summary: Congress mps and senior leaders raised slogans against pm narendra modi and the bharatiya janata party over the neet paper leak. Rahul said that he marched to pm modi's residence to demand answers for the brutalities against students on monday. Union minister jitendra singh claims rahul changed demands mid-negotiation.

Article Title: Rahul dragged, Priyanka clings to bus door: Five pictures that defined Congress protest outside PM Modi's residence
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi being taken away by police

Mallikarjun Kharge

Rahul Gandhi

Rahul Gandhi, Jitendra Singh, Govind Mohan

Rahul Gandhi

Rahul Gandhi

Priyanka Gandhi Vadra

NEW DELHI: High drama unfolded outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence in Delhi on Tuesday as the Congress, led by Rahul Gandhi, arrived to stage a sit-in protest, catching security personnel off guard.The Congress leaders demanded the resignation of PM Modi, Union home minister Amit Shah, and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Rahul Gandhi also appealed to the public to join what was planned as an indefinite protest, following which leaders from other opposition parties joined the demonstration.Congress leaders tied plastic bands around their hands and raised slogans, protesting the government's action.The government sent Union minister Jitendra Singh and home secretary Govind Mohan to urge Rahul Gandhi and the Congress to call off the protest, citing the high-security nature of the area. However, the request was rejected.Singh claimed that he informed Rahul Gandhi that the government had agreed to discuss the NEET paper leak issue in Parliament, following which the Congress MP "changed the goalpost" and demanded the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan.Eventually, Delhi Police detained Rahul Gandhi and other leaders participating in the protest.The Congress claimed that Rahul Gandhi sustained a nose injury while in Delhi Police custody.Meanwhile, party MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra attempted to resist her detention by clinging to the door of a bus in which protesters were being taken away.The dramatic protest came a day after Delhi Police and other security forces stopped a march by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) to Parliament, with protesters demanding the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET (UG) paper leak.The CJP march, held on the first day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, turned violent, leaving both protesters and security personnel injured.
Article Summary: Congress leaders demand resignation of pm modi, home minister amit shah, education minister dharmendra pradhan. Delhi police detain rahul gandhi and other leaders participating in the protest. Party mp priyanka gandhi vadra attempts to resist her detention by clinging to a bus in which protesters were being taken away.

Article Title: Assam flood toll rises: 21 killed in a day; over 5.6 lakh affected across 16 districts
Article Text:
A resident sits on the steps of a flood-affected house surrounded by inundated water

NEW DELHI: At least 21 people were killed in Assam in the last 24 hours as the flood situation worsened, with more than 5.64 lakh people affected across 16 districts, according to the Assam State Disaster Management Authority (ASDMA).The latest fatalities included 13 deaths in Sivasagar, five in Charaideo, two in Golaghat and one in Jorhat, the ASDMA said in its late-night bulletin.With the latest deaths, the overall flood toll this year has risen to 31.The number of affected people increased sharply from 3.63 lakh in 15 districts on Monday to over 5.64 lakh. Sivasagar remained the worst-hit district, with 3.59 lakh people affected, followed by Jorhat (87,662) and Charaideo (72,646).A total of 872 villages across 44 revenue circles remained inundated, while 71 relief camps sheltering 12,284 displaced people and 202 relief distribution centres are currently operational.Chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said he would visit the worst-affected districts on Wednesday to review relief and rescue operations."The recent #AssamFloods, caused by the cloudburst upstream, have brought immense hardship to our people.Tomorrow I will be visiting flood-affected areas and relief camps... to review the relief operations," Sarma said in a post on X.Several rivers, including the Dikhou, Disang and Brahmaputra, continued to flow above the danger mark or highest flood level at multiple locations. Rescue operations are being carried out by the Air Force, NDRF, SDRF and Fire and Emergency Services.Train services remained disrupted for the second consecutive day after floodwaters submerged railway tracks in parts of Sivasagar district. The Northeast Frontier Railway has cancelled, diverted or short-terminated several trains and is operating special services for stranded passengers.The Department of Telecommunications has also activated the Intra-Circle Roaming (ICR) facility in Sivasagar, Charaideo and Jorhat until July 24 to ensure uninterrupted mobile connectivity in the flood-hit areas.
Article Summary: The latest fatalities included 13 deaths in sivasagar, five in charaideo, two in golaghat and one in jorhat. With the latest deaths, the overall flood toll this year has risen to 31. Chief minister himanta biswa sarma will visit the worst-affected districts on wednesday.

Article Title: Watch: Rahul Gandhi dragged away, forcibly removed from protest site; pics show nosebleed
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi being dragged away by security officials

How the protest unfolded

Government's response

NEW DELHI: Congress on Tuesday released videos showing dramatic scenes of detentions of senior party leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra during party’s ‘Chalo PM House’ protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence over the NEET issue.The videos captured the moments when police moved in to clear the protest outside the Prime Minister's residence.In one video shared by the Congress on its official X handle, leader of opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi was seen lying on the road while police personnel attempted to lift him and remove him from the protest site.Former Congress president appeared to resist being taken away as security officials surrounded him and tried to carry him from the spot.In another video, he can be seen being dragged by the police.The party also released photograph showing Gandhi with what appeared to be a nosebleed following the police action.In a separate video, he can be seen lying down during the protest and talking to reporters, taunting the government by saying, "Democracy in India is happening right now."Another video released by the party showed Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra standing at the entrance of a police bus and refusing to board it.Officials are seen pulling her inside the vehicle as she resisted being detained.The Congress also shared several photographs on its official social media handles showing Rahul Gandhi and other party leaders being removed by police during the protest.The Congress has presented the videos and photographs as evidence of the manner in which its senior leaders were detained during the protest.Delhi Police forcibly removed and detained Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and several other opposition leaders after they staged a sit-in outside the Prime Minister's residence.Rahul Gandhi, along with Congress leaders including the chief ministers of Karnataka and Kerala, began the protest at around 3.30 pm against the police action on students protesting the previous day.The protest came after Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, met students injured during Monday's clashes linked to the CJP-led march over the NEET issue.The Congress has been demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan and a discussion on the NEET issue in Parliament.The party has also sought justice for students injured during the police action.Meanwhile, Union minister of state in the Prime Minister's Office Jitendra Singh said he met Rahul Gandhi at the protest site along with Union home secretary Govind Mohan and urged him to end the sit-in.Singh claimed Rahul Gandhi initially agreed to withdraw the protest if the government allowed a discussion on the NEET issue in Parliament.He alleged that after the government conveyed its willingness to hold the discussion, Gandhi added a fresh demand seeking the resignation of the education minister. Singh described the Congress leader's conduct as "unfortunate and contrary to every norm of democracy."Congress leaders, however, have maintained that they were protesting against the alleged police action on students and accused the government of refusing to address the issue in Parliament.
Article Summary: Congress releases videos showing dramatic scenes of detentions of senior party leaders rahul gandhi and priyanka gandhi vadra. The videos captured the moments when police moved in to clear the protest outside the prime minister's residence. The party also released photograph showing gandhi with what appeared to be a nosebleed following the police action.
